code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test21-OCT-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly low serum glucose, elevated creatinine, and mild hyperkalemia, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction (possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy). No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and glycemic status.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
